titleStmt.title: SII 26.290: original edition by P. R. Srinivasan
No. 290. (A.R. No. 275 of 1909.) TIRUKKACHCHŪR, CHINGLEPUT TALUK, CHINGLEPUT DISTRICT.

summary.p: This inscription records a gift of 32 cows and a bull to provide daily one uḻakku of ghee for burning a nandā lamp in the temple of Tiruvālakkōyiluḍaiyār at Tirukkachchūr as occurring in No. 289 above, by one Kulōttuṅgaśōḻakkaṇṇappaṉ-Ādināyaṉ Pañchanadivāṇaṉ alias Rājarāja Nīlagaṅgaraiyaṉ of Tiruchchuram in Kulōttuṅgaśōḻa-vaḷanāḍu in Puliyūr-koṭṭam in Jayaṅgoṇḍa śōḻamaṇḍalam. The cows and the bull were received by some Śivabrāhmaṇas of the temple, who had agreed to burn the lamp perpetually.
A postscript at the end of the inscription states that in the 18th (year of king ?), a standing lamp weighing 132 palam was given to enable the burning of the nandā lamp mentioned above.

revisionDesc.change: Conversion of digital encoding to DHARMA encoding scheme according to EGD (Encoding Guide for Diplomatic Editions)

div.p:
svastiśrī . tiruvāykkeḻvi muṉpāka maturaiyumiḻamum pāṇṭiyaṉai muṭittalaiyuṅ koṇṭaruḷiṉa tribhuvanaccakravattikaḷ śrīkulottuṅkacoḻadevaṟku yāṇṭu
10 3 Āvatu jayaṅkoṇṭacoḻamaṇṭalattu kaḷattūṟkoṭṭattuc ceṅkuṉṟanāṭṭut tirukkaccūr Āḷuṭaiyār tiruvālakkoyiluṭaiyāṟku Immaṇṭala
ttup puliyūrkoṭṭattu kulontuṅkacoḻavaḷanāṭṭu tiruccurattu kulottuṅkacoḻakkaṇṇappaṉātināyaṉ pañcanativāṇaṉāṉa rājarāja nilaṅkaraiyaṉ tiru
vālakkoyiluṭaiyāṟku vaitta tirununtā viḷakkoṉṟukku nāḷoṉṟukku neyyuḻakkukkup poliyūṭṭu cantrādityavarai celuttak kaṭavomāka Ikkoyiliṟkāṇi
yuṭaiya śivabrāhmaṇaril bhāradvājikādvikkuṟi śrī bhaṭṭaṉ kkāśyapan Uṭaiyāṉāṉa kākunāmabhaṭṭaṉ
rukkāḷatti Uṭaiyāṉāṉa Uruttiracivabhaṭṭanum Ivvaṉaivom Ivar pakkal kaikkoṇṭa pacu muppattiraṇṭum ṛṣabhamoṉṟum kaikkoṇṭu Ittirunantā viḷakku Oṉṟum candrādityavarai
Erikkakkaṭavom Ivvaṉaivom Itu paṉmāheśvararakṣai .. Ittevarkku pati ṉeṭṭāvatu Ittirunantāviḷakku Erikka Iṭṭa Ittirukkuttiviḷakku Oṉṟiṉāl tarā
Eṭai nuṟṟu muppattiru palam paṉmāheśvararakṣai §
Digital edition of SII 26.290 by automatically converted to DHARMA conventions.
